custom.name: auto-session4-no-silent-build
custom.message: Learned from session 4: Silent build failures hide missing tools. Let builds fail loudly or check at runtime with which.

custom.name: auto-session4-pydantic-schema-sync
custom.message: Learned from session 4: Verify Pydantic response schemas include all ORM model fields. Missing extracted_path hid the action buttons.

custom.name: auto-session5-no-scalar-one-or-none-on-fk
custom.message: Learned from session 5: scalar_one_or_none() crashes with MultipleResultsFound on one-to-many relations. Use .order_by().limit(1) or .all() instead.

custom.name: auto-session5-yara-no-unreferenced-strings
custom.message: Learned from session 5: Every YARA string must appear in the condition. Unreferenced strings cause compilation errors.

custom.name: auto-review-no-raw-join-in-sandbox
custom.message: Learned from review S13: Use validate_path() instead of os.path.join for paths under firmware root. Raw join follows symlinks without sandbox checks.

custom.name: auto-review-no-shell-interpolation
custom.message: Learned from review S2/S4: Use shlex.quote() for all variables interpolated into shell commands. Prefer create_subprocess_exec (list form) over sh -c.

custom.name: auto-review-no-commit-in-tool-handler
custom.message: Learned from review K1: Use context.db.flush() in tool handlers. The outer MCP dispatch handles commit — committing here prevents rollback on partial failures.

custom.name: auto-standalone-dockerignore-venv
custom.message: Learned from standalone binary campaign: Ensure .dockerignore excludes .venv -- COPY . . will overwrite the Docker-built venv with the host venv, causing Python version mismatches and missing modules.

custom.name: auto-standalone-lief-enum-case
custom.message: Learned from standalone binary campaign: LIEF Mach-O CPU_TYPE enums are UPPERCASE (X86_64, ARM64), not lowercase. Always verify with dir().

custom.name: auto-session19-venv-which
custom.message: Learned from session 19: In Docker with uv/venv, CLI tools from pip packages land in /app/.venv/bin/ not system PATH. Use: shutil.which(tool) or shutil.which(tool, path="/app/.venv/bin")

custom.name: auto-docker-opt-verify-ldd
custom.message: Learned from docker-image-optimization: Verify runtime deps with ldd before trusting comments about which packages are needed. Comments rot faster than code.

custom.name: auto-fwid-api-function-missing-firmwareId
custom.message: Learned from S37 firmware-id bug: Every file API function must accept firmwareId param since the backend resolve_firmware dependency uses it for scoping.

custom.name: auto-s37-case-insensitive-fnmatch
custom.message: Learned from S37: User-facing file search should be case-insensitive. Use fnmatch.fnmatch(name.lower(), pattern.lower()) instead.

custom.name: auto-extraction-roots-no-direct-extracted-path
custom.message: Learned from feature-extraction-integrity (CLAUDE.md rule 16): Filesystem walks MUST use get_detection_roots(firmware) from app/services/firmware_paths.py - firmware.extracted_path is ONE rootfs and misses scatter-zip siblings, multi-archive containers, and nested unblob output. Per-binary flows (emulation/fuzzing/device/sandbox) may still use extracted_path. Originally uncovered by DPCS10 md1dsp.img bug + RespArray ZIP regression.

custom.name: auto-classifier-magic-offset-beyond-buffer
custom.message: Learned from feature-classifier-patterns-mcu-kernel: The detector reads only detector._MAGIC_READ_BYTES (64) bytes into `magic`. Indexing past 0x40 (64) silently fails to match. If a new format needs a deeper header check (e.g. iMX-RT BOOT_DATA @ 0x1000), widen _MAGIC_READ_BYTES with a rationale OR route through a per-format parser in parsers/, do not add an unreachable index.

custom.name: auto-intake-sweep-1-no-stat-docker-sock
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep Phase 1 (commit bac49ea, /health/deep regression 29dba35): stat-based /var/run/docker.sock presence checks fail after the docker-socket-proxy migration because the raw socket is no longer mounted. Probe via get_docker_client().containers.list(limit=1) instead -- verifies BOTH DOCKER_HOST connectivity AND the proxy CONTAINERS allowlist.

custom.name: auto-intake-sweep-1-no-docker-from-env
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep Phase 1 Stream D (commit bac49ea -- 10 sites across 8 files migrated): use get_docker_client() from app.utils.docker_client instead of docker.from_env(). The factory honours settings.docker_host which routes through the socket proxy; direct from_env() bypasses the proxy and breaks emulation/fuzzing/terminal flows once the raw socket mount is removed.

custom.name: auto-wave3-pydantic-extra-ignore-on-api-schemas
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep Wave 3 stream beta (DeviceInfo BROM bug): Pydantic extra='ignore' on API-boundary schemas silently strips fields sent by the service layer -- DeviceInfo dropped 4 BROM fields, surfacing as (dev as any).mode === undefined at runtime with zero compile-time signal. Prefer extra='forbid' (loud drift) or explicit field declarations. Use extra='allow' only for ingestion DTOs with a documented reason.

custom.name: auto-wave3-frontend-tsc-no-noemit
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep Waves 1+2+3 (CLAUDE.md Rule #24): wairz frontend tsconfig uses files: [] + project references; tsc --noEmit exits 0 silently without descending into referenced projects (Rule #17 silent-CLI-exit instance). Use npx tsc -b --force. Rule-17 canary mandatory once per session before trusting any green typecheck output: write a known-bad .ts file, run the command, confirm it fails.

custom.name: auto-wave3-intake-yaml-status-lowercase
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep Wave 3 stream gamma: intake YAML status: values should be lowercase (completed, pending, partial, draft, active) for close-out query coherence. Capitalised values work but break case-sensitive greps (grep -l 'status: completed' misses capital-C entries; close-out queries must use grep -li). Status-bump should land in the same commit that ships the intake's work, not wait for a maintenance-sweep.

custom.name: auto-fleet-worktree-requires-worktree-add
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep sessions 435cb5c2+198243b8 (CLAUDE.md Rule #23 refined by Wave-3 evidence): git checkout -b feat/{branch} ALONE is insufficient under shared on-disk checkout -- alpha and gamma saw 4 cross-stream commit-sweep incidents across 14 commits when they only used branch-checkout. Beta had 0 sweeps in 4 commits AFTER running git worktree add .worktrees/stream-{name} -b feat/stream-{name}-{date} and operating inside that path. Each parallel sub-agent prompt MUST issue git worktree add (not just git checkout -b) and operate inside .worktrees/<stream>/. The Fleet harness isolation: worktree sentinel is a no-op at the working-tree level.
